mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction in expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the consumer price index above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of months when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness, which reduces aggregate income and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following housing starts increases, or the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 105 signals supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, which increases input costs and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered observations during late-cycle expansions, or the signal shows no predictive edge in regime-filtered walk-forward tests.
